title: Countries of Europe
about: This map is drawn from Natural Earth 1:50m public-domain country boundaries, re-projected with an Azimuthal Equal-Area projection centred on Europe and cropped to a European window — so the eastern extents of Russia and Kazakhstan are clipped by a straight edge rather than shown in full.

It covers the 45 sovereign states commonly counted as European (including transcontinental ones such as Russia, Turkey, Cyprus and the Caucasus), plus six microstates shown for learning but not quizzed. Outlines are simplified for display and are not authoritative.

Some borders and the status of certain territories are disputed — for example Kosovo, Crimea, Northern Cyprus, Abkhazia, South Ossetia, Transnistria and Nagorno-Karabakh. Their depiction follows the Natural Earth source and is not intended as a political statement.
